content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff and the PID-liveness implementation paths. I found two high-severity correctness gaps: same-second PID reuse is still unsafe for marker-only non-Linux records, and failed/paused recovery can still resume or reclaim past live legacy pid-only orchestrators because those paths do not consistently consult the new live-state logic.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: On macOS/BSD, marker-only `run.pid` records still falsely match a reused PID that starts in the same second, so `run stop`/liveness checks can target an unrelated process.
severity: high
affected_files: src/adapters/fs.rs:262-310, src/adapters/fs.rs:814-823, src/cli/run.rs:2698-2777
remediation_hint: When `proc_start_marker` is the only identity, do not treat a raw marker match as definitively live; combine it with `record.started_at`/`compare_legacy_pid_record` and downgrade same-second matches to `RunningUnverified` instead of `Live`.
   Rationale: `ps -o lstart` is only second-granular. The new legacy-path ambiguity handling exists, but authoritative marker-only records bypass it and go straight through `is_pid_alive()`, so same-second PID reuse on non-Linux is still misclassified as the original process.
2. finding_summary: Failed/paused recovery still ignores a live legacy pid-only orchestrator when there is no current owner metadata, and it can also reclaim a stale owner based only on lease age, allowing resume/recovery to run alongside the original process.
severity: high
affected_files: src/cli/run.rs:1840-1872, src/cli/run.rs:1924-1938, src/cli/run.rs:2015-2030, src/cli/run.rs:3525-3539
remediation_hint: Before reclaiming or acquiring the recovery writer guard for failed/paused snapshots, consult `FileSystem::pid_record_live_state`; `Live` and `RunningUnverified` legacy pid records should block resume/reclaim the same way the running-snapshot and interrupted-handoff paths now do.
   Rationale: For failed/paused snapshots, `observed_resume_recovery_owner()` only harvests `writer_owner`/lock owners. A legacy `run.pid` without owner metadata yields `observed_owner: None`, and the subsequent recovery lock acquisition proceeds without any PID liveness check. Even when an owner is present, `observed_stale_running_owner_still_matches_attempt()` only treats authoritative records as live, so a stale lease can still reclaim a live legacy pid-only owner.
